entry.title: SAP Practice and Certification Journey
entry.content:
In today’s fast-evolving logistics industry, digital transformation and integrated systems play a critical role in ensuring efficiency, transparency, and sustainability. As someone who has received formal education in logistics and is actively continuing my professional development in this field, I strongly believe that learning SAP S/4HANA Transportation Management (TM) is a natural and strategic step in my career journey.

My motivation to learn SAP stems from my desire to deepen my understanding of logistics beyond operational processes and into system-driven decision making. Transportation Management is one of the core pillars of supply chain operations, and SAP S/4HANA TM provides an end-to-end perspective—from planning and execution to cost settlement and performance analysis. Gaining hands-on practice with this system will allow me to better understand how logistics processes are designed, optimized, and managed within global organizations.

Additionally, learning SAP S/4HANA TM will help me develop a more analytical and structured mindset. Exposure to different ERP systems not only enhances technical competence but also enables logistics professionals to think holistically, connecting operations, finance, and strategy. I believe this knowledge will significantly contribute to my ability to analyze complex logistics scenarios and propose effective, data-driven solutions.

Through SAP Practice and Certification, my goal is to strengthen my professional skill set, bridge the gap between theory and real-world applications, and position myself as a more capable and future-ready logistics profession

entry.title: Accelerating SAP Digital Manufacturing with SAP Test Automation by Tricentis in SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
In today’s fast‑moving manufacturing landscape, standing still is not an option. With quarterly SAP Digital Manufacturing releases, evolving shop‑floor processes, and constant pressure to scale, maintaining a stable and reliable system becomes a challenge. Every rollout, upgrade, or plant onboarding demands repeated testing and when done manually, this quickly turns into a time‑consuming bottleneck. 

The good news? SAP Test Automation solution by Tricentis allows you to accelerate delivery, tighten quality, and free your team from repetitive validation work, and many SAP customers can get started using tools they already own. Whether you’re operating one plant or fifty, automation helps ensure your SAP Digital Manufacturing processes run smoothly, release after release.

 

Why automate testing in SAP Digital Manufacturing?

Across SAP Digital Manufacturing implementations, similar challenges appear:
manual regression cycles are time‑intensive, multiple process variants must be validated repeatedly, and quarterly SAP releases require consistent checks to ensure integrations and shop floor processes behave as expected.

Automation tackles these pain points by:

  • Reducing repetitive manual testing, especially across multiple plants
  • Shortening regression cycles, enabling faster rollout of new features
  • Increasing stability and coverage, even as processes evolve
  • Freeing business users for meaningful testing, not repetitive validation

Instead of re-running known scenarios every quarter, teams can focus their time on what’s new or business‑critical.

🧰 What’s in your toolkit? (You already own it!)

If you have SAP Enterprise Support, cloud edition(s), you likely already have access to:

SAP Cloud ALM

Your central hub for test preparation, planning, orchestration, and defect management.
✔ Included in your subscription
✔ Up to 24 GB data

For more information, please refer to usage rights for SAP Cloud ALM.

SAP Test Automation solution by Tricentis

Included entitlement with:

✔ 5 named users

✔ 5 execution agents

✔ 500 automated test runs/month

✔ 2.5 GB storage

To learn more, please refer to usage rights for SAP Test Automation solution by Tricentis

The setup is done very easily. Tricentis integration in SAP Cloud ALM can be ready in just minutes. Click here to learn more.

 

From manual to automated: How it works

1️⃣ Document scenarios in SAP Cloud ALM

  • Capture end‑to‑end flows, from order creation all the way to shop‑floor execution.

2️⃣ Mark tests as automated

  • SAP Cloud ALM directly links your test cases to Tricentis for execution.

3️⃣Scan and build automation in Tricentis

  • Capture Operator PODs and SAP DM UI elements
  • Build automation flows with drag‑and‑drop modules
  • Parameterize your data for reuse across plants, variants, and releases

4️⃣ Execute & analyze

  • Run your automated tests from Tricentis or SAP Cloud ALM
  • Review logs, screenshots, and step‑by‑step recordings
  • Track defects and trigger re‑runs directly in SAP Cloud ALM

 

Best practices

  • Begin with stable, repetitive processes

  • Combine UI automation with API validations
  • Use parameterized data — avoid hard-coded materials/SFCs
  • Keep automation updated with each release
  • Assign clear ownership for automation assets

 

Common pitfalls when starting test automation

Implementing SAP Test Automation in SAP Digital Manufacturing is not only a technical task. It requires the right mix of business process knowledge, test methodology expertise, and automation skills. A small, dedicated expert team is essential to set the foundation correctly from the start, and many organizations benefit from engaging specialized partners from SAP’s growing test automation ecosystem.

There are several pitfalls that frequently occur when customers begin their automation journey — from organizational setup to preparation and script implementation. A few key ones include:

🎯 Starting automation without a clear test strategy or SAP DM–specific objectives
Ensure clarity on what you want to achieve, how testing will be organized, and which processes automation must protect.

👥 Assigning automation solely to business users
Testing requires dedicated ownership; business experts contribute scenarios, but automation should be owned and maintained by a skilled team.

🌱 Trying to automate everything at once
Start small, focus on stable and high‑value scenarios, and grow automation iteratively.

🧩 Using static or hard‑coded data
This limits flexibility across plants, variants, and releases, and reduces confidence in test results.

These represent only a subset of common challenges. We will continue expanding this guidance, and customers are encouraged to collaborate with partners who bring proven methodology and automation experience.

 

Further resources

  • A beginner session on ensuring quality at speed with SAP Cloud ALM and Tricentis testing solutions
  • Webcast recording: Cloud ALM for Implementation of SAP Digital Manufacturing (2024)
  • Try out our SAP Cloud ALM public demo tenant 
  • Learn more about SAP Cloud ALM 

 

What's next?

To help you build a strong SAP Test Automation foundation in SAP Digital Manufacturing and make the most of it, we will be launching a dedicated webcast series soon. Stay tuned, more details are coming soon! 🚀

entry.title: Characteristics-Based Planning: Shelf Life Meets Compliance
entry.content:
The Problem: When Systems Can't See What Matters

In pharmaceutical, food and beverage, and consumer goods industries, not all units are equal. Products cannot be any more treated as homogeneous SKUs. Batches have remaining shelf life. They carry regulatory approvals for specific markets. They age every day the planning system runs

In regulated industries, batches within the same SKU aren't interchangeable. A pharmaceutical batch with 60 days of remaining shelf life approved for US and EU markets is not the same as a batch with 90 days remaining approved only for EU. They cannot be treated equally in planning.

The result? Planners export data to spreadsheets, manually check approval matrices and expiration dates, then adjust plans accordingly. When demand changes or new batches arrive, the process repeats.

The consequences cascade differently across organizations:

Business leaders see the waste-compliance squeeze. Conservative planners avoid compliance risks by scrapping usable inventory. Aggressive planners maximize utilization but this can trigger audit findings

IT teams inherit the integration burden. ERP systems track batch expiry dates. Regulatory/Quality management systems hold approval records. Planning generates demand plans. Someone has to connect these further into feasible Supply!

What's Changed:

CBP with enhanced Shelf-Life planning is now available for SAP7F and Harmonized Planning Area (IBP2)!! 

Release 2602 marks a significant step forward. Characteristics-Based Planning (CBP) is now available on the Harmonized planning Area and we've fused the two critical dimensions: Flexibility of CBP and shelf life management --> unified with regulatory approval constraints.

With this here is what you get: 

Guaranteed Compliance: Regulatory + Quality(Shelf-Life) constraints evaluated simultaneously - no more choosing between fresh or compliant.

IBP now plans at the batch + CBP characteristic level together. It understands that Batch A has 60 days remaining shelf life and is approved for EU and US markets, while Batch B has 100 days remaining but only US approval. When US demand arrives requiring minimum shelf life of  80 days, the system automatically routes Batch B to this demand - all within the standard planning run.

No spreadsheet exports. No manual approval checking. No custom code calculating time decay.

The technical challenge this solves is significant: as you add characteristics (regulatory approvals, quality grades) with Shelf life, the combinatorial complexity explodes. Planning must evaluate every supply against every demand requirement while time continues to erode shelf life. The system handles all constraints natively, during your regular planning cycles.

Other Benefits

  • Enhanced Shelf-Life Planning capabilities
  • Ability to use CBP and Enhanced Shelf-Life planning independently or together!
  • For, SAP7F, enable the enhanced function by setting a global configuration parameter 
  • For HPA, only the new enhanced Shelf-Life solution will be available – No special parameter for activation

Enhanced Shelf-Life Planning capabilities include:

  • improved precision with Lot Sizing: Old Min/Max building versus new lot grouping id as used for CBP
  • Shelf-Life Propagation to components and inheritance: Finished product’s usable life is constrained by the remaining Shelf-Life of a critical lower-level BoM component (currently restricted to one component)
  • there's more to come in subsequent releases

Business Outcomes:

Executives can finally eliminate the waste-versus-compliance tradeoff. The organization reduces spoilage while maintaining its compliance posture. Supply chain leaders reclaim planner productivity. Teams stop spending hours manually routing batches and start focusing on exception management and strategic decisions. IT organizations operate using standard SAP capabilities including integration to ERP --> TCO drop potential.

Whats Next: 

Shelf life planning with regulatory compliance is available now in SAP IBP 2602. Watch our demo video to it in action, or connect with your SAP team to assess fit for your organization.

Video: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=tFybO70a0Ls 

This release aligns into our harmonized ways for planning and paves the way forward for subsequent releases where we build on this for some complex use cases. Stay tuned!

This is the third blog in the series: First two blogs can be found here: 

Unlock the Potential of Characteristics-Based Planning (CBP) in SAP IBP

Breaking the Silos:Orchestrate end to end Characteristics Based Plans across Planning and Execution

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

entry.title: Accelerate Your Role in SAP Supply Chain Transformation: Join Cloud Acceleration Partner Sessions
entry.content:
Today’s supply chains are evolving faster than ever—and SAP’s customers are moving with urgency to modernize both their manufacturing and planning landscapes. To help partners lead these critical transformations, SAP has offered two dedicated sessions that introduce the SAP Supply Chain Management Evolution Program, designed to equip you with the insight and confidence needed to guide customers through their next steps.

Why These Sessions Matter

Across manufacturing and planning, SAP customers are shifting from long‑established on‑premise solutions to the next generation of cloud‑based platforms:

  • SAP ME/MII → SAP Digital Manufacturing
  • SAP APO and ERP-based planning → SAP Integrated Business Planning (SAP IBP)

Both transformations represent major opportunities for partners. But they also require a clear understanding of the Customer Evolution Program—SAP’s structured approach to helping customers modernize with minimal risk and maximum long‑term value.

 What You’ll Gain by Watching the Replay:

Each session is designed to give partners a practical understanding of:

  • How SAP is guiding customers through cloud transformation in manufacturing and planning
  • The purpose and structure of the SAP Supply Chain Management Evolution Program
  • The vital role partners play in helping customers adopt SAP Digital Manufacturing and SAP IBP
  • Real examples of customer transformation journeys and what drives success
  • Where partners can access SAP topic experts, resources, enablement, and next steps

By reviewing, you’ll leave with a clearer view of how to position the program with your customers—and how to identify opportunities to accelerate their transformation journey.

  • SAP ME/MII → SAP Digital Manufacturing
  • SAP APO/ERP → SAP Integrated Business Planning

These sessions are your opportunity to understand how SAP is helping customers evolve—and how you, as a partner, can play a central role in that journey.

entry.title: Welcome to our Supply Chain Management Learning Group!
entry.content:
WELCOME!

We are very excited to launch this brand-new learning group for all learners of SAP solutions in the Supply Chain space.

This is a space where learning, collaboration, and real-world supply chain experience come together — a place where ideas grow, questions are welcome, and learning becomes a shared journey rather than a solo effort. Whether you ask questions, share insights, or simply follow along and learn at your own pace, you are part of this community.

Here you can connect with SAP experts and fellow learners across all SAP Supply Chain topics and be among the first to hear about new courses, live sessions, and certification updates.

The focus of this community is learning. It complements the existing SAP product communities, which remain the right place for detailed product discussions and system troubleshooting. Here, the emphasis is on understanding concepts, developing skills, and progressing along your SAP Supply Chain learning journey.

This is a safe space to discuss SAP learning content such as courses, learning journeys, hands-on exercises, and certifications. No question is too basic — we are all learners here. Questions related to learning systems and training exercises are very welcome.

The community is moderated by SAP experts who are involved in creating and delivering official learning content and live sessions, so you are connecting directly with the team behind the training.

The solutions that we plan to cover in this community include:

  • Production Planning
  • Manufacturing & Digital Manufacturing
  • Project Systems & Portfolio and Project Management
  • Extended Warehouse Management
  • Variant Configuration
  • Product Engineering

Before you leave, make sure to click Join Group so you can ask questions, participate in discussions, and share your thoughts. Also remember to subscribe to the learning community so you don’t miss any updates.

 

SAP Community provides a separate place for discussions and questions, but we thought we’d try and simply things for you by combining them. After all, a question often leads to a discussion and vice-versa. So please use the Discussions button to start a discussion and to ask questions.

 

Why not introduce yourself to members of this community and let us know a little about your SAP journey? What brought you here? What are you hoping to get from this community? Simply post a reply here and let us know.

Make sure you spread the word about this community. Post a link in your social media so that we can build a vibrant and effective community of data and analytics learners.

As with all SAP communities, we want this to be a safe space for our members. Please familiarize yourself with the SAP Community Rules of Engagement

Let’s connect, learn, and grow together –  we look forward to building this Supply Chain learning community with you.

entry.title: Upcoming: SAP Solutions for PLM Info Days 2026 (Virtual Event)
entry.content:
Let’s Spread the Word: SAP Solutions for PLM Info Days 2026 

From February 24–26, 2026, SAP will host the SAP Solutions for PLM Info Days as a three-day virtual event. The event will showcase the latest innovations and solution highlights across the SAP PLM portfolio, with a strong focus on collaboration, efficiency, and digital transformation.

Agenda overview:

  • Day 1 – Feb 24, 2026: PLM solutions for discrete industries

  • Day 2 – Feb 25, 2026: PLM solutions for process industries

  • Day 3 – Feb 26, 2026: Project & Portfolio Management

Participants will also have the opportunity to connect directly with SAP experts and gain insights into current developments and best practices.

The virtual format also allows for flexible participation — you can join for all three days or attend selected sessions that best fit your interests and schedule.

If you are interested, you can register for the event here. 

This is a valuable opportunity to expand your knowledge and engage with SAP experts. We look forward to your participation!

entry.title: New Certification Available: SAP Certified Associate – SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, Production
entry.content:
New Certification Available: SAP Certified Associate – SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, Production

We are excited to announce that the new certification SAP Certified Associate – SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, Production Planning and Manufacturing is now live.

This certification is designed for consultants working in the SAP S/4HANA Manufacturing environment and validates core skills required to actively contribute to implementation projects. It applies to both SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and SAP S/4HANA on-premise solutions.

What This Certification Confirms

The certification verifies that candidates have a solid understanding of:

  • Manufacturing processes in SAP S/4HANA

  • Production Planning

  • Production execution and shop floor processes

  • Material components and master data

It demonstrates both business process knowledge and technical capabilities relevant for real-world project environments.

Recommended Learning Journey

To prepare for the certification, SAP offers the learning journey:

Implementing Manufacturing in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition

This comprehensive program covers:

  • Core manufacturing business processes

  • Production Planning fundamentals

  • Advanced Planning with PP/DS

  • Shopfloor Control for discrete and process industries

  • From master data setup to production order execution

  • Capacity planning and optimization

The learning journey provides structured guidance and hands-on knowledge to ensure participants are fully prepared for the certification exam.

Staying Certified

SAP certifications are valid for 12 months.
To keep your certification active, you can extend it by successfully completing the required assessment before expiration. SAP provides personalized notifications to help you stay up to date.

Continuous learning and regular certification updates ensure that your skills remain aligned with the latest SAP innovations.

Get Started

You can find detailed information about the certification here:

entry.title: Public Cloud Release 2602 Enhancement: Goods Movement Posting from Manage Reservation Items App
entry.content:
S/4 HANA Public Cloud Release 2602 Enhancement: Direct Goods Movement Posting from Manage Reservation Items App

Introduction

             In warehouse and production processes, material movements such as Goods Receipt (101), Goods Issue (201, 261) and Transfer Posting (311) are commonly executed against reservations in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud.

             After the 2602 upgrade in my public cloud system, I explored the enhancement made to the Manage Reservation Items app. The update allows goods movements to be posted directly against reservations from the same app, without navigating to a separate goods movement application.

Scenario:

            To understand the behavior in detail, I tested the functionality using movement type 311. I created a manual reservation for intra-plant stock transfer and processed it directly through the Manage Reservation Items app to verify how the system handles the posting.

Example:

Plant

From Storage Location

To Storage Location

1000

RM Store

Production Store

Before Release 2602 – My Observation

           

           Before the 2602 upgrade, when I created a reservation (either manually or through MRP), the goods movement could not be posted directly from the reservation screen.

            After creating the reservation, I had to navigate to the separate Goods Movement app, manually enter movement type 311, reference the reservation, and then post the document.

           From a user perspective, this involved switching between apps and entering data again, which increased the chances of manual errors. For warehouse users handling multiple reservations, this process was slightly time-consuming.

What I Observed After Release 2602

           After the 2602 upgrade, I tested the same scenario again. I noticed that the Manage Reservation Items app now allows direct posting of goods movements against the reservation itself. I was able to select the reservation and create the goods movement without navigating to another app. The system automatically generated the material document once the posting was completed.

           This change reduces navigation steps and makes the process more convenient for warehouse users.

Step 1:

           Create a manual reservation using movement type 311 in Manage Reservation Items app.

         Below screen shot of Process Flow

Step 2:

         Select the created reservation (001), choose "Create Goods Movement", and post the document.

Benefits:

         Multiple reservation items can be processed in a single posting. Partial quantity posting is supported.

         Below is the material document generated through the "Manage Reservation Items" app for your reference.

Conclusion:

                  The enhancement in Release 2602 enables warehouse users to post goods movements (Goods Issue and Goods Receipt) directly from the Manage Reservation Items app against manual and MRP-generated reservations. This reduces manual effort, eliminates app switching, and improves operational efficiency.

entry.title: What's New in SAP S/4HANA 2025 - Advance Available to Promise (aATP) Business Uses and Configuration
entry.content:
1. Product Availability Check (PAC) Enhancements in SAP S/4HANA 2025

1.1 Flexible Reservation & Locking Strategies

PAC now supports three locking approaches:

What’s new: PAC now supports three locking approaches:

  • Optimistic reservation
    • Temporary confirmation without immediate DB lock
    • Lock applied only during final commit
    • Improves performance for high-volume order entry
  • Exclusive locking
    • Locks supply immediately during check
    • Prevents parallel confirmations consuming same stock
    • Best for scarce or regulated inventory
  • Automatic selection
    • System dynamically chooses locking logic

Business scenario example:

  • E-commerce bulk orders → Optimistic locking improves response time
  • Aerospace spare parts → Exclusive locking ensures accuracy

Configuration steps:

SPRO → Sales and Distribution → Advanced ATP → Product Availability Check → Configure Reservation / Locking

Fiori apps applicable:

  • Configure Product Availability Check
  • Check Product Availability
  • Review Availability Check Result

1.2 Enhanced Scope of Check Logic

Bucket Logic Options

  • Progressive → confirms earliest feasible supply (may split dates)
  • Conservative → confirms only if full quantity available

Check Without Batch

ATP ignores batch segmentation during check.

When it’s useful:

  • Batch assigned later by warehouse
  • Quality inspection determines batch
  • Batch irrelevant at order stage

Subcontracting Supply/Demand Handling

PAC now better accounts for:

  • Components issued to subcontractor
  • Expected finished-goods receipt

Configuration steps:

SPRO → SD → Advanced ATP → Product Availability Check → Define Scope of Check

Fiori apps applicable:

  • Configure Product Availability Check
  • Check Product Availability

1.3 Horizon for Late Supply

What it does: If supply arrives later than expected:

  • Confirmation can shift automatically
  • Within configured tolerance window

Prevents unnecessary:

  • Cancellation
  • Rescheduling
  • Customer communication noise

Example:

Production delay = 2 days
Tolerance horizon = 3 days
Confirmation auto-adjusted

Configuration steps

SPRO → SD → Advanced ATP → Product Availability Check → Maintain Check Horizon

1.4 Automatic Quantity Rounding

New behavior in SAP S/4HANA 2025:

Confirmed quantities validated against:

  • Delivery unit
  • Packaging rules
  • Rounding profile

Example:

Order: 97 units
Delivery unit: 10
Confirmation: 90 units

Configuration steps:

SPRO → SD → Advanced ATP → Product Availability Check → Maintain Rounding Rules

2. Backorder Processing (BOP) Enhancements in SAP S/4HANA 2025

2.1 Availability Change Log (ACL)

What’s new - System logs every event affecting availability:

  • Goods receipts
  • Production completion
  • Purchase order updates
  • Allocation changes
  • Supply protection updates

Result - BOP now:

  • Reprocesses only impacted orders
  • Runs faster
  • Supports near-real-time prioritization

Configuration steps:

SPRO → SD → Advanced ATP → Backorder Processing → Activate Availability Change Log

Fiori apps applicable:

  • Backorder Processing
  • Backorder Processing Monitor

2.2 Event-Driven Availability Updates (RAP Integration)

aATP now aligns with modern ABAP RESTful event handling:

  • Cleaner extensibility model
  • Supports automation workflows
  • Enables responsive ATP recalculation

Useful for:

  • Custom logic
  • Cloud extensions
  • API-driven processes

2.3 Interactive Backorder Processing Improvements

Major capabilities - Business can now:

  • Drag-and-drop reorder priorities
  • Upload worklists
  • Override allocation/supply protection temporarily
  • Fix confirmation date or quantity
  • Process orders by segment

Business value - Planner can:

  • Instantly prioritize VIP customer
  • Reclaim supply from low-priority order
  • Confirm urgent shipment in seconds

Configuration Steps:

SPRO → SD → Advanced ATP → Backorder Processing → Define BOP Segments & Strategies

Fiori Apps applicable:

  • Manage Confirmations for Backorders

3. Alternative-Based Confirmation (ABC) Enhancements in SAP S/4HANA 2025

3.1 Storage Location Constraint

ABC strategy can now enforce:

  • Confirmation must consider original storage location or
  • Restrict substitution to allowed locations

Useful for:

  • Regulated goods
  • Ownership-based stock
  • Quality-specific inventory

3.2 Storage Location Constraint New Rating Attributes

Maximum Sequence Number: Limits number of alternative sources checked.

Minimum Consumption Priority: Ensures preferred sources used first.

Benefit - Improves:

  • Performance
  • Sourcing efficiency
  • Predictability

3.3 Maximum Partial Deliveries Control

System respects limit on: number of partial shipments

Avoids:

  • Excessive delivery fragmentation
  • Logistics complexity

3.4 Enhanced Logging & BAdI

Logging: Improved traceability in SLG1.

BAdI: BADI_ATP_ABC_ENRCD_SUBSTN_RSLT

Allows:

  • Dynamic substitution logic
  • Attribute modification

Configuration Steps:

SPRO → SD → Advanced ATP → Alternative-Based Confirmation → Maintain Strategies

Fiori apps applicable:

  • Configure Alternative-Based Confirmation
  • Review Availability Check Result

4. Third-Party Order Processing Integration in SAP S/4HANA 2025

4.1 Availability-Controlled Purchasing

ABC can now dynamically do the following:

If internal supply insufficient → evaluate vendor availability → confirm via third-party sourcing.

Supported scenarios

  • Vendor → Customer (drop-ship)
  • Vendor → Internal plant → Customer

4.2 Backorder Follow-Up Strategies

Shortage quantity can automatically and reduces manual interventions:

  • Trigger supplier sourcing
  • Convert into third-party fulfillment

4.3 Unified Confirmation Visibility

Third-party confirmations appear in: Review Availability Check Result

Business sees:

  • Internal supply result
  • Supplier confirmation
  • Substitution logic

4.4 Business Process Scheduling Integration

Custom scheduling schemas allow:

  • Supplier-specific lead time calculation
  • Dynamic ETA determination

Configuration steps:

SPRO → Cross-Application Components → Business Process Scheduling

Fiori apps applicable:

  • Review Availability Check Result
  • Display Sales Order Fulfillment Issues

5. Automatic STO Creation (Plant Consolidation) in SAP S/4HANA 2025

5.1 How it works in SAP S/4HANA 2025

If: Supplying plant ≠ Customer-facing plant

ABC can:

  • Create Stock Transport Order automatically
  • Move stock internally
  • Confirm delivery from customer plant

Business impact

Without consolidation:

  • Multiple plants ship separately

With consolidation:

  • Single shipment
  • Reduced freight cost
  • Simplified delivery process

Configuration steps:

SPRO → SD → Advanced ATP → Alternative-Based Confirmation → Configure Plant Substitution / STO Creation

Fiori apps applicable:

  • Review Availability Check Result
  • Manage Stock Transfer Orders
  • Manage Sales Orders

6. Product Allocation (PAL) in SAP S/4HANA 2025

6.1 Flexible Allocation Periods

Previously

Rigid weekly/monthly buckets.

Now

You can:

  • Create any duration period
  • Auto-generate period sequences
  • Upload/download planning data
  • Migrate existing objects

Business Example:

  • 5-day launch period
  • 12-day seasonal period

Configuration Steps:

SPRO → SD → Advanced ATP → Product Allocation → Maintain Allocation Objects & Period Types

Fiori apps applicable:

  • Manage Product Allocation Planning Data
  • Monitor Product Allocation

6.2 CDS Extraction Views for Analytics

Planning Data available in:

I_PRODALLOCOBJTIMESERIESDEX

Consumption Data available in:

I_PRODALLOCQTYASSIGNMENTDEX

Enables below view:

  • SAC dashboards
  • Predictive analysis
  • Custom reporting

7. Supply Protection (SUP) in SAP S/4HANA 2025

7.1 Resolve Overprotection App

Problem addressed: Protection rules can unintentionally block all confirmations, resulting in no quantities being confirmed for sales or delivery requests.

The new app helps you proactively identify issues by detecting:
• Protection objects that are fully blocked and preventing confirmations
• Conflicting or overlapping protection rules causing unintended restrictions
• Situations that could lead to zero confirmations, impacting order fulfillment and customer commitments

7.2 Automated Mitigation Suggestions

Priority-based protection reduction: The system evaluates protection hierarchy and rule priority in aATP and proposes decreasing protected quantities at selected priority levels to resolve confirmation shortages while maintaining higher-priority allocations.

Lowest-segment protection optimization: Identifies protection segments (customer/channel/region) with surplus reserved stock and recommends reducing protection in the lowest-priority segment to release supply with minimal impact on strategic demand.

Restricted-quantity recalibration: Analyzes protection object limits and confirmation logic, suggesting adjustments to restricted quantities or release thresholds so that available stock can participate in ATP checks and avoid zero-confirmation outcomes.

7.3 Situation Management Alerts

Automatic planner notification: When aATP detects a supply–demand imbalance (for example, protection-rule conflicts, shortages, or zero-confirmation risk), a Situation Management alert is generated and automatically assigned to the responsible planner via the situation template and recipient determination.

Event-driven alert creation: The system monitors predefined ATP-relevant conditions and thresholds; once violated, it creates a situation instance with contextual data (product, location, order, confirmation status) for immediate analysis and action.

Workflow-triggered response: The situation can automatically trigger follow-up workflows—such as task creation, email/Fiori notification, or escalation—enabling planners to review protection settings, adjust allocations, or re-run confirmation checks without manual monitoring.

Configuration Steps:

SPRO → SD → Advanced ATP → Supply Protection → Maintain Protection Objects & Groups

Fiori apps applicable:

  • Resolve Overprotection
  • Monitor Supply Protection

8. Supply-Creation Based Confirmation in SAP S/4HANA 2025

8.1 Length-Based ATP

Functionality

  • System can confirm orders using longer available material, planning cutting/splitting to meet requested length.
  • Tolerance rules allow acceptable over-length or deviation during confirmation.
  • Supports automatic feasible confirmation instead of rejection when exact length stock is unavailable.
  • Helps optimize material utilization, reduce scrap, and minimize manual planner intervention.

Delivered Technical Objects

  • Planning Procedure: 7 – activates length-oriented planning logic in PP/DS.
  • Heuristic: SAP_LEN_001N – standard heuristic for:
    • Length-based planning
    • Cut optimization scenarios
    • Tolerance-based confirmations
    • Allocation of variable-dimension stock

Configuration steps

  • SPRO → Production Planning → PP/DS → Define Planning Procedures & Heuristics

8.2 Characteristics-Based Availability Check

Integration Capabilities

  • ATP triggers PP/DS-based evaluation to validate:
    • Configuration characteristics – ensures selected variant or options are manufacturable.
    • Production feasibility – checks capacity, resources, and material availability in detailed scheduling.
    • Optional supply creation – system can automatically generate planned orders or procurement proposals if supply is missing.

Delivered Technical Objects

  • Planning Procedure: 6 – enables PP/DS-supported supply check during ATP confirmation.
  • Heuristic: SAP_PP_SBC_R – used for:
    • Supply-based confirmation logic
    • Automatic planned order creation
    • Feasibility-oriented production scheduling

Fiori apps applicable:

  • Check Product Availability
  • Schedule Production
  • Manage Planned Orders

Conclusion:

I would say, SAP S/4HANA 2025 aATP release delivers meaningful technical enhancements that improve the robustness and intelligence of the order confirmation process across planning, allocation, and execution layers:

  • Automation → Automated STO creation and refined sourcing logic improve supply selection, reduce manual intervention, and ensure consistent confirmation behavior during ATP checks.
  • Control → Enhancements to Interactive BOP and overprotection resolution provide finer control over confirmation redistribution, enabling planners to rebalance supply based on priority, rules, and real-time availability.
  • Flexibility → Extended allocation-period handling and enriched substitution attributes allow more granular product/location substitution and time-phased protection management aligned with business constraints.
  • Manufacturing integration → PP-based ATP improvements strengthen the linkage between production supply (planned/process orders, receipts, capacity-relevant dates) and confirmation logic, resulting in more accurate commit dates.

From a system and process perspective, these capabilities help organizations with complex, multi-node fulfillment networks achieve:

  • More deterministic and reliable confirmations through improved rule evaluation and supply matching
  • Increased real-time supply transparency across allocations, protection objects, and production receipts
  • Higher planning and logistics efficiency via reduced manual BOP runs and exception handling
  • Improved service levels and customer promise accuracy driven by tighter integration between ATP, allocation, and manufacturing data

Overall, the 2025 release continues the evolution of aATP toward a proactive, exception-driven confirmation framework, enabling supply chain teams to move from reactive troubleshooting to controlled, automated order promising.

If you want to configuration steps, you will get the detail step by step configuration of SAP S/4HANA 2025 aATP on SAP help  - https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/f132c385e0234fe68ae9ff35b2da178c/a004ec57a7b5bc12e10000000a4450e5.html 

Also you can read summary of changes in SAP S/4HANA 2025 aATP written by Sujeet Acharya (SAP Product Lead) -  https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blog-posts-by-sap/release-highlights-of-advanced-available-to-promise-aatp-in-sap-cloud-erp/ba-p/14240423

entry.title: Automated Invoice and Credit Memo Processing Using ERS in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Automated Invoice and Credit Memo Processing Using ERS in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud

Introduction

In a standard procurement process, materials are procured from a supplier through purchase order creation, goods receipt posting, invoice processing, and vendor payment. When Evaluated Receipt Settlement (ERS) is activated, the system automatically creates the supplier invoice based on the goods receipt, eliminating the need for manual invoice posting.

During one of the SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud implementations, a requirement came up to handle supplier return scenarios using ERS. In cases where materials were found defective after receipt and payment, the business expected the system to automatically generate a credit memo once the returned goods were posted. This blog explains the end-to-end process that was configured and tested in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud to achieve this automation.

Step 1: Maintain Business Partner Master Data

 Use the Manage Business Partner Master Data app.

Ensure the following fields are enabled for the Supplier in the Purchasing tab:

  • Automatic Evaluated Receipt Settlement
  • Evaluated Receipt Settlement
  • Goods Receipt-Based Invoice Verification

These settings allow the system to generate invoices and credit memos automatically without manual invoice posting.

Step 1: Image 1

Step 2: Schedule ERS Background Job 

Use the app: Schedule Supplier Invoice Jobs – Advanced

Step 2: Image 1

Select the job template: Evaluated Receipt Settlement

This job automatically:

  • Creates Supplier Invoices for GR
  • Creates Credit Memos for Returns

I have created scheduled background jobs for ERS using this app.

Step 2: Image 2

 

Step 2: Image 3

Step 2: Image 4

Step 2: Image 5

Step 3: Create Standard Purchase Order (ERS Enabled)

Create a standard PO.

Ensure:

 ERS checkbox is automatically enabled in the Invoice tab.

Step 3: Image 1

Post Goods Receipt (GRN).

Once the scheduled ERS job runs: System automatically generates the Supplier Invoice document. The below image shows the PO history reflecting the automatically created invoice document.

Step 3: Image 2

In the Schedule Supplier Invoice Jobs – Advanced app, the below image shows the scheduled job that automatically generated the supplier invoice based on the ERS configuration.

Step 3: Image 3

Step 3: Image 4

Note: In SAP S/4HANA Cloud (Public Edition), the classic GUI transaction MRRL used for Evaluated Receipt Settlement is not available. Instead, ERS processing is executed through the Schedule Supplier Invoice Jobs - Advanced app.

Step 4: Vendor Payment

Based on the automatically generated invoice:

  • Vendor payment is processed and cleared using standard payment process.

Return Process Using ERS

Step 5: Create Return Purchase Order

When defective material needs to be returned:

  • Create PO with Return Indicator
  • Ensure ERS is enabled in the PO

Step 5: Image 1

Step 6: Post Return Goods Receipt

Post Goods Receipt for the Return PO.

Once the scheduled ERS job runs:

  • System automatically generates a Credit Memo
  • No manual invoice posting required
  • Liability is adjusted automatically

Step 6: Image 1

In the Schedule Supplier Invoice Jobs – Advanced app, the below image shows the scheduled job that automatically generated the credit memo for the supplier return process based on the ERS configuration.

Step 6: Image 2

Step 6: Image 3

Conclusion

In my experience, ERS in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud works smoothly not only for regular procurement but also for supplier return scenarios, provided the configuration is done correctly. Once the supplier master is properly maintained and the background job is scheduled, the system automatically takes care of invoice generation for goods receipts and credit memo creation for returns.

The key is to ensure that ERS is consistently enabled in both the standard and return purchase orders. When everything is aligned, the process runs without manual intervention, reducing effort for the finance team and avoiding posting errors. This makes the overall P2P cycle more controlled and efficient.

entry.title: SAP Core Logistics for SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition, 2025 FPS00 & FPS01 – What’s New
entry.content:
We are proud to introduce the first-ever “What’s New” release blog for Core Logistics in SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud, highlighting the 2025 FPS00 and FPS01 releases. This update has been a long time coming, and it reflects sustained commitment across our teams to bring durable, enterprise-grade capabilities to our customers. Despite significant challenges over the past years, our dedicated engineering teams have persisted in delivering a set of high-quality features and enhancements.

Core Logistics provides the foundational execution and planning capabilities that underpin end-to-end logistics processes in SAP S/4HANA. Within the broader SAP S/4HANA Supply Chain portfolio, Core Logistics sits at the center — providing the essential, reliable core that enables advanced planning, warehouse management, transportation management and industry-specific extensions to work together seamlessly. The 2025 release enhances that foundation with targeted improvements designed to increase operational resilience, simplify integration, and improve day-to-day usability for logistics professionals.

In the following sections, we’ll highlight the key innovations in this release, show how they deliver real-world value to customers, and provide resources to support implementation and adoption. We’re excited to share this milestone and grateful to the teams and customers who helped make it possible.

As I missed publishing this piece alongside the initial SAP S/4HANA 2025 FPS00 release, and with the recent release of FPS01, I shall combine both releases into one single blog. And we shall begin this overview with the highlights delivered in FPS00.

One more important thing to note is that I shall group the delivered innovations according to their corresponding solution capability. For those new to Logistics Execution – or as a quick refresher - here are the key capability categories we’ll be exploring:

  • Delivery Management – a component of SAP Logistics Execution, Delivery Management is an important part of the logistics chain in which guaranteed customer service and distribution planning support play major roles. In Delivery Management, all delivery procedure decisions can be made at the start of the process by: (1) Considering general business agreements with your customer; (2) Recording special material requests; (3) Defining shipping conditions in the sales order. The result is an efficient and largely automatic shipping process in which manual changes are only necessary under certain circumstances.
  • Handling Unit Management – used to map packing-controlled logistics in the SAP system, HU Management does not consider the movements of the individual materials, instead it maps the movements of the handling units that contain these materials. Available in all core logistic areas where logistic material bookings can take place, this feature uses handling units to map logistical processes to simplify the processing of goods movements thus optimizing logistical processes. This simplified process includes the existing packing function across core logistics and the storage unit processing in the Warehouse Management system.
  • Inventory Management - Inventory Management is part of the Materials Management module and is fully integrated into the entire logistics system. Material is procured from external or internal sources based on the requirements determined by Material Requirements Planning. The delivery is entered in Inventory Management as a goods receipt. The material is stored (and managed under Inventory Management) until it is delivered to customers (Sales & Distribution), or is used for internal purposes (for example, for production).

CORE LOGISTICS IN SAP S/4HANA 2025 FPS00

Delivery Management

Supply to Customer (S2C) Integration

The first highlight I want to mention is one tightly linked to EWM. This is a foundational update which will allow users to send deliveries that are relevant for the Supply to Customer (S2C) scenario to EWM. Just-in-Time (JIT) processing is a common practice in manufacturing industries for efficient demand-driven production and logistics across supply chains. Just-In-Time Supply to Customer covers JIT processes from the perspective of a supplier. This update for Delivery Management enables production warehouse operators to work with the supply-to-customer (S2C) process for Just-In-Sequence (JIS) calls when using Extended Warehouse Management (EWM) embedded in SAP S/4HANA in their storage locations.

After the production and packing of the sequenced component groups, the system creates outbound deliveries using the Managed Customer JIT Calls by Component Groups app, including handling unit (HU) numbers from (multi-level) packing. These deliveries are then transferred to EWM. PLEASE NOTE that on the EWM side this feature will be enabled as of the SAP S/4HANA 2025 FPS01 release from February 2026.

Customizing updates in Delivery Management

In Customizing activity Activate Decentralized Warehouse System, users can choose how SAP S/4HANA communicates with the external Warehouse Management system. In addition, customizing activity of Activate Service-Based Communication has been removed.

Furthermore, Activate Decentralized Warehouse System has been extended with the table column Comm. External WM. In this new column, users can select SOAP Web Services to enable the external warehouse management services. This configuration was previously made in Customizing activity Activate Service-Based Communication, which has been removed to avoid redundancy.

Inventory Management

OData API: Material Document - Read, Create

The OData API Material Document - Read, Create (API_MATERIAL_DOCUMENT) has been enhanced with the following new fields:

  • ReservationItemRecordType - In the case of a discontinued material in a reservation from a production order, the value provided here indicates whether the discontinued material or the follow-up material should be consumed (“[   ]” = The discontinued material should be consumed; “1” = The follow-up material should be consumed)
  • BatchbySupplier - The Supplier Batch field has been added. This is the number used to manage the batch on the supplier’s side.

SOAP API: Material Document – Create

The SOAP API Material Document - Create (MaterialDocumentCreateRequest_Async_In) has been enhanced with a new field ReservationItemRecordType. For reservations coming from production orders, it is now possible to indicate whether to use a discontinued material or its follow-up material when the goods movement are created (“[   ]” = The discontinued material should be consumed; “1” = The follow-up material should be consumed).

Handling Unit Management

Enabling Auxiliary Packaging Material in 3PL SOAP APIs

Third-party warehouse management APIs enable data exchange with warehouse management systems in external systems. The main use of these APIs is to communicate with third-party warehouse systems and logistics providers. These services can be used to send and receive goods from the external warehouse. In addition, it's possible to update and split outgoing deliveries.

With the SAP S/4HANA 2025 FPS00 release, the 3rd Party Logistics SOAP APIs has been enhanced to capture secondary packaging information in the field AdditionalPackaging within the Handling unit node. The following SOAP APIs have been updated to support this enhancement:

  • CO_LEIDW_WAREHOUSE_SHIP_ORDER
  • WAREHOUSEINTERMEDIATEUPDATE_IN
  • WAREHOUSESTOCKTRANSFERRECEIPTA
  • CO_LEIDW_WAREHOUSE_STOCK_TRANS
  • WAREHOUSESHIPPINGADVICE_IN

Both Customizing activities can be accessed in transaction SPRO under Logistics Execution à Integration of External Warehouse Systems.

Enabling Key User Extensibility at the Handling Unit Header in 3PL SOAP APIs

Another enhancement for 3rd Party Logistics APIs is that key user extensibility was enabled at the Handling Unit header in the 3PL SOAP APIs used for external warehouse management. The following SOAP APIs were enabled to support custom fields creation at Handling Unit header via the Key User Extensibility app:

  • CO_LEIDW_WAREHOUSE_SHIP_ORDER
  • WAREHOUSEINTERMEDIATEUPDATE_IN
  • WAREHOUSESTOCKTRANSFERRECEIPTA
  • CO_LEIDW_WAREHOUSE_STOCK_TRANS
  • WAREHOUSESHIPPINGADVICE_IN

To use these services, they must first be configured in the S/4HANA system. For this, SAP provides a services group and AIF scenario. For more information about configuring the services in the SOA system, see the related sources for External Warehouse Management Services and for Central Configuration.

Enhancement of Handling Unit API with ISO Codes for Units of Measurement

 A final highlight worth mentioning is the enhancement of the Handling Unit (HU) API, which now includes new fields to support ISO-compliant units of measurement.

 At the Handling Unit header level, the following fields have been enabled to support ISO codes for units of measurement.

:

Field name 

Description 

HndlgUnitTareVolumeUnitISOCode

Tare volume unit of measure of a handling unit in ISO code.

HndlgUnitTareVolumeUnitSAPCode

Tare volume unit of measure of a handling unit in SAP code.

HndlgUnitTareWeightUnitISOCode

Tare weight unit of measure of a handling unit in ISO code.

HndlgUnitTareWeightUnitSAPCode

Tare weight unit of measure of a handling unit in SAP code.

UnitOfMeasureDimensionISOCode

Dimension unit of measure in ISO code.

UnitOfMeasureDimensionSAPCode

Dimension unit of measure in SAP code.

VolumeUnitISOCode

Volume unit of measure ISO code.

VolumeUnitSAPCode

Volume unit of measure in SAP code.

WeightUnitISOCode

Weight unit of measure ISO code.

WeightUnitSAPCode

Weight unit of measure of in SAP code.

 

 At the Handling Unit item level, the following fields have been enabled to support ISO codes for units of measurement

:

Field name 

Description 

HndlgUnitQuantityUnitISOCode

Quantity unit of a handling unit in ISO Code.

HndlgUnitQuantityUnitSAPCode

Quantity unit of a handling unit in SAP Code.

 

CORE LOGISTICS IN SAP S/4HANA 2025 FPS01

Delivery Management

Persistence of Asset Postings in Delivery Structure

With this feature, we have smoothed the process of managing assets across multiple decentralized warehouse locations. Previously, when assets were moved between decentralized warehouses, the asset reference information would sometimes get lost in translation during the delivery process. Consequently, without clear asset identification, the system would throw posting errors, forcing users to manually trace which specific company asset was being moved.

With the SAP S/4HANA 2025 FPS01 release, asset references can be embedded directly into delivery documents from the get-go. Now, when an asset moves through the warehouse network, its unique identifier travels with it every step of the way, from the initial goods receipt to the final goods issue posting. For logistics managers, this translates to fewer system interruptions, reduced manual intervention, and most importantly, reliable asset tracking across your entire warehouse ecosystem.

To enable this feature, the delivery communication structure KOMDLGN is extended with the asset fields ANLN1 (main asset number) and ANLN2 (asset subnumber). These fields are populated from the goods movement data (MSEG) during delivery generation and persist in the delivery item data (LIPS). During post goods issue, the same fields are mapped back from the delivery to the goods movement interface to ensure that the asset information is available for final posting.

Enhanced Transfer of Control Dates to support Multistage Intercompany Sales and Stock Transfer Processes

The Transfer of Control (ToC) refers to the specific point in time when ownership or responsibility for goods officially shifts from one party to another. This is critical for revenue recognition and accounting purposes, as it determines when a sale can be recorded and when inventory responsibility changes hands.

When dealing with multistage intercompany sales or stock transfers, such as when goods pass through multiple legal entities before reaching their destination, tracking exactly when control transfers at each handoff point becomes exponentially more complicated.

To support multistage intercompany sales and stock transfer process, the Transfer of Control (ToC) Dates in the outbound delivery header has been enhanced by introducing four new internal ToC-Date groups directly in the header. These additional fields provide the granularity needed to precisely coordinate and document each transfer of control event as goods move between the various organizations involved in your multistage process. Whether you’re managing a three-way intercompany sale or routing stock through regional distribution entities, you now have the tools to accurately capture each ownership transition along the journey.

For more information on Multistage Intercompany Sales process and the Multistage Intercompany Stock Transfer process, please read the following Help Portal documentation:

  • Multistage Intercompany Sales
  • Multistage Intercompany Stock Transfer

Other Delivery Management feature enrichments

And lastly with regards to Delivery Management enhancements delivered with the SAP S/4HANA 2025 FPS01 release, I would like to briefly mention the following extensibility updates:

  • RAP Object updates – two RAP Business Objects (BO) available in Delivery Management have been updated. For the RAP BO I_INBOUNDDELIVERYTP we enabled editing of the Putaway Quantity field, as well as enabled the use of the PostPartialGoodsReceipt action for inbound deliveries. For the I_OUTBOUNDDELIVERYTP RAP BO, the Picking Quantity field is now editable. Furthermore, RAP events are now called when a delivery is assigned to or removed from a shipment using transaction Create Shipment (VT01N) or Change Shipment (VT02N). This allows you to effectively use the BO with shipments.
  • Extensibility for Goods Movements – with this release we also allow users now to extend and change data in a delivery before goods movement is posted. For this, the Business Add-In Goods Movement Data Modification (LE_SHP_SPE_GM_CREATE) can be used. The BAdI can be found in the Customizing (SPRO) for Logistics Execution (LE) under Extended Warehouse Management Integration > Transfer and Inventory Management > Business Add-Ins (BAdIs).

For more information on SAP’s Clean Core strategy, and how our extensibility updates factor into it, please read the Clean Core extensibility white paper.

Inventory Management

Prevention of Goods Receipt Posting for Purchase Order Items with 'Delivery Completed' Indicator Set

This feature introduces a safeguard that prevents goods receipts from being posted against purchase order items already marked with the 'Delivery Completed' indicator (field ELIKZ). When an attempt is made to post a goods receipt for such items, the system displays a customizable message. If the message category is configured as 'Error Message', the system will halt the goods receipt posting entirely for any items where the 'Delivery Completed' indicator is set.

This enhancement is particularly valuable for organizations looking to maintain tighter control over their inventory records. It eliminates the risk of inadvertently posting additional receipts against purchase order lines that have already been closed out – a scenario that can lead to inventory discrepancies and reconciliation headaches down the line.

On the configuration side, administrators have full control over how the system responds to these posting attempts. A new message (message class: M7, message number: 506) has been introduced in customizing table T160M specifically for this purpose. The message category options include 'No Message', 'Information', 'Warning', and 'Error Message', with 'No Message' set as the default. For organisations wanting to enforce strict compliance and prevent any goods receipts against completed items, setting the message category to 'Error Message' will ensure the system blocks these postings outright.

Support for Multistage Intercompany Sales and Stock Transfer Processes in Inventory Management

Inventory Management (MM-IM) receives a significant capability boost with this release, now fully supporting goods issue and goods receipt processes within multistage intercompany sales and stock transfer scenarios.

The Customizing activity 'Assign Movement Types to Value Chain Process Steps' has been expanded to accommodate these multistage scenarios. Dedicated movement types are now available specifically for multistage intercompany sales and stock transfers, and administrators have the flexibility to customize these movement types to align with their organization’s specific operational requirements.

Furthermore, the system now handles timing mismatches in the transfer process. In real-world operations, goods don’t always move in a perfectly sequential manner – sometimes an inbound delivery gets confirmed at the receiving plant before all intermediate steps in the chain have been completed. Previously, this could cause system errors and processing bottlenecks. Now, the system allows temporary negative Valuated Stock in Transit at the receiving plant when goods transfer occurs ahead of goods receipt posting.

Warehouse Handling Unit field in Material Documents Overview

This release brings improved traceability for handling unit information directly into core inventory documentation. The Material Documents Overview app (App ID: F1077) now includes a dedicated Warehouse Handling Unit field (Technical name: EWM_HUIDENT, Type: /SCWM/DE_HUIDENT), giving users immediate visibility into handling unit details without needing to navigate between multiple screens or systems.

Behind the scenes, several objects have been enhanced to support the persistence of Warehouse Handling Unit Identifiers within Material Documents. This means that when handling unit information flows in from Extended Warehouse Management (EWM), the system now retains that data directly in the material document record rather than losing it in translation between systems.

BAPI function modules that have been enhanced with warehouse handling unit information:

  • MAP2I_B2017_GM_ITEM_TO_IMSEG
  • MP2E_IMSEG_TO_2017_ITEM_CREATE

The IDoc Segment E1BP2017_GM_ITEM_CREATE1 has also been enhanced.

Furthermore, two Core Data Services (CDS) views in Inventory have been updated to include the Warehouse HU field, namely the Material Document Item view (I_MATERIALDOCUMENTITEM_2) and the Goods Movement view (I_GOODSMOVEMENTDOCUMENT).

The Warehouse Handling Unit field is populated in the material document only when it is explicitly passed through the BAPI, IDoc, or OData interfaces.

Other Core Logistics Updates

And since the Core Data Services view for Material Document Item has already been mentioned, I_MATERIALDOCUMENTITEM_2 has been enhanced with service document fields, namely:

  • ServiceDocument
  • ServiceDocumentItem
  • ServiceDocumentType

Lastly, I want to go over two Extensibility updates that were delivered with this release:

  • The OData API ‘Material Document - Read, Create’ (API_MATERIAL_DOCUMENT) has been enhanced with Warehouse Handling Unit field. More specifically, the ‘Document Items’ entity contains a new property, ‘HandlingUnitExternalID’.
  • Key user extensibility has been enabled for the Packing Instruction Header through SAP GUI and Odata API. Users can create custom fields for packing instruction table PACKKP using business context PACKINGINSTRUCTION via Key user extensibility. The created fields can be managed via SAP GUI transactions POP1/POP2/POP3, as well as via the via the OData API API_PACKINGINSTRUCTION.

Artificial Intelligence: Using Joule in batch management

With the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, 2025 FPS01 release, batch management gets a modern, conversational upgrade with this release through Joule integration. Users can now query batch master information using natural language, making navigation easier and more intuitive. Simply ask Joule to search the batch required for your need, using the batch master attributes, Joule fetches the batch master information based on the criteria provided in the prompt.

The natural language queries support several key fields, including batch number, material, plants, batch status, manufacturing date, shelf-life expiry date, and supplier. This makes it easier to pull up batch information whenever needed, such as to check expiry dates for quality purposes or verify supplier details for traceability requirements.

When deeper analysis or updates are required, Joule provides seamless navigation directly to the batch master object page within the Manage Batches app. From there, users can explore additional batch details, modify information as needed, or take further actions. This integration represents a step forward in making everyday inventory tasks more accessible and efficient through AI-assisted interaction.

FIND OUT MORE!

You can also find more technical details and documentation on the above listed topic and others we left out via the “What’s New” viewer on the Help Documentation portal.

For more information on Multistage Intercompany Sales process and the Multistage Intercompany Stock Transfer process, please read the following Help Portal documentation:

  • Multistage Intercompany Sales
  • Multistage Intercompany Stock Transfer

For more information on SAP’s Clean Core strategy please read the Clean Core extensibility white paper.

entry.title: Automatic Batch Determination Using FEFO for Transfer Posting (Movement 311) in SAP Public Cloud
entry.content:
Automatic Batch Determination Using FEFO for Transfer Posting in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud

Introduction

  • In industries such as pharmaceuticals, food, chemicals, and FMCG, managing material shelf life is critical. Organizations must ensure that materials with the earliest expiration dates are consumed or transferred first to prevent wastage.
  • SAP provides a First Expiry First Out (FEFO) strategy through Batch Determination to automatically select batches based on their expiration dates.
  • This blog explains how to configure and use Automatic Batch Determination using FEFO for Transfer Posting (Movement Type 311) in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud.

Step 1 – Create Strategy Type

  • To enable batch determination for transfer posting, first create a Strategy Type for Inventory Management.

Menu Path

IMG  -> General Business Data ->  Batch Management -> Batch Determination -> Batch Determination for Inventory Management -> Define Inventory Management Strategy Types

Image 1-1

  • A new strategy type “ZB12” was created to enable FEFO-based batch determination.

       Image 1-2

Image 1-3

Step 2: Create Search Procedures

Image 2-1

  • A new search procedure “ZB0003” was created and the strategy type “ZB12” was assigned to it.

Image 2-2

Step 3- Allocate IM Search Procedure/Activate Check:

  • Assigned search procedure “ZB0003” to movement type 311.

Image 3-1

Step 4- Create Sort Sequence:

  • A new sort sequence “ZFEFO1” was created and the characteristic “LOBM_VFDAT” (Expiration Date) was assigned to ensure batches are sorted in ascending order based on the earliest expiry date, enabling the FEFO strategy.

Image 4-1

Image 4-2

Step 5- Create Class

  • Two classes were created: “ZBATCH_FEFO1” for assigning to the material in the classification view and “FEFOCLASS” for use in the batch search strategy.

Image 5-1: “ZBATCH_FEF0”

  • Assigned the class to material “FEFO2”.

Image 5-2:

Step 6: Manage Search Strategies – Inventory Management:

Image 6-1

  • In “Manage Search Strategies – Inventory Management”, the strategy was assigned for movement type 311 and class “FEFOCLASS” was maintained.

Image 6-2

Image 6-3

  • Assigned the sort sequence “ZFEFO1” to enable FEFO-based batch sorting.

Image 6-4

Step 7: Maintain Shelf Life Data in the Material Master

Scenario Testing:

Below is the screenshot of Stock Overview for the following:

Material: FEFO2

The stock overview displays batch-wise details including:

  • Production Date
  • SLED (Shelf Life Expiration Date)

This information helps the system determine the appropriate batch during FEFO-based batch determination, ensuring that batches with the earliest expiry date are selected first.

Image 8-1

Testing – Transfer of 10 Quantity

A stock transfer was executed:

  • Movement Type: 311
  • From Storage Location: 1001
  • To Storage Location: 1003
  • Quantity: 10

After clicking the Batch Determination icon:

  • The system automatically assigned the batch.
  • The batch selected was the one with the earliest expiry date, as per FEFO logic.
  • The expiry date was displayed during batch selection.

Image 8-2

Testing with Increased Quantity (20 Qty)

Further testing was performed by increasing the quantity to 20.

Since:

  • The first expiry batch “FEFO567” had only 10 quantity available,

After clicking the Batch Determination icon:

  • The system automatically allocated
  • 10 Qty from Batch FEFO567 (earliest expiry batch)
  • Remaining quantity from the next earliest expiry batch

This confirms that the system splits the quantity automatically based on FEFO logic.

Conclusion:

The FEFO concept is functioning as expected during stock transfer using movement type 311.

  • Batch determination correctly selects the batch with the earliest expiry date based on the FEFO principle.
  • Expired batches are not considered during batch determination when the remaining shelf life condition is maintained.
  • The system automatically splits quantities across multiple batches when the required quantity exceeds the available stock in the first batch.

entry.title: Use case: Event-Driven Integration of Robotic Tire Palletizing with Cimcorp, MES, and SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
Author: Naveen Chandra Sharma

(Naveen is a SAP Solution architect having 29+ years of experience which includes 20+ years into SAP consulting with expertise in SAP ECC, SAP S/4HANA and integration with other external system like MES, CIMPRO etc. He worked on various type of project flavors like Implementation, Enhancement and support projects in various industries Manufacturing, Finance, Pharmaceutical, Food, Telecommunication)

 1.Abstract:

This use case considers a tire manufacturing environment in which finished tires moves down through a conveyor channel to a warehouse staging area. Within the staging area, multiple robotic units are installed to automatically pick the individual tires from the conveyor and load them onto pallets. The robotic palletizing process continues until the pallet reaches its predefined capacity limit. Once the pallet is fully loaded, it is transferred to the end of the staging area where it becomes available for warehouse handling operations. A warehouse operator subsequently retrieves the completed pallet and executes the put away process within the warehouse management system, ensuring proper storage and inventory registration.

The overall system landscape consists of four primary systems: SAP ECC, SAP S/4HANA with decentralized Extended Warehouse Management (EWM), the Manufacturing Execution System (MES), and the Cimcorp warehouse automation system. SAP PI/PO serves as the middleware layer to enable communication and data exchange between MES and the decentralized S/4HANA EWM system. This document presents a structured architecture describing the integration and interaction among the aforementioned systems.

Above architecture works well with event driven integration model, which ensures the transactional consistency between MES, CIMPRO and EWM system during whole tire palletization process.

2.0 Introduction:

Example: The tire production line operates at a very high speed and runs continuously 24×7 at full capacity. On average, one tire moves to the staging area every two seconds, resulting in approximately 30 tires per minute. Since the staging area has limited space, automation becomes essential to handle the high production rate efficiently. Without automated handling and palletizing systems, tires would quickly accumulate in the staging area, leading to operational congestion and potential disruption of the production flow.

Another approach would be going without structured integration, robotic systems often operate as isolated system, which restricts real-time inventory visibility and reconciliation challenges between production systems and Warehouse system.

This case study proposes a standardized integration framework connecting a Cimcorp robotic palletizing system with a Manufacturing Execution System (MES) and SAP S/4HANA integrated with embedded SAP Extended Warehouse Management (EWM). This architecture enables the following:

• Real-time pallets build confirmation
• Pallet handling unit creation
• Serial-level traceability for tiers
• Goods receipt automation
• Warehouse bin put-away
• End-to-end production-to-warehouse synchronization

The proposed design supports scalable automation environments commonly found in modern tire manufacturing plants.

3.0 System Architecture:

a. Enterprise Resource Planning Layer (ERP Layer)

This layer manages enterprise-wide business processes and master data.

Systems:

  • SAP ECC – Legacy ERP system responsible for financials, material master data, sales orders, and enterprise reporting.

Key Responsibilities

  • Material master and business master data management
  • Financial and sales transactions
  • Inventory visibility at enterprise level
  • Integration with warehouse and production systems

b. Warehouse Management Layer

This layer handles warehouse execution and logistics operations.

System:

  • SAP S/4HANA with Decentralized Extended Warehouse Management (EWM)

Key Responsibilities

  • Warehouse task creation and management
  • Inventory tracking and stock movements
  • Put away and storage bin management
  • Handling unit and pallet management
  • Integration with warehouse automation systems

c. Manufacturing Execution Layer

This layer manages shop-floor production control and operational execution.

System:

  • Manufacturing Execution System (MES)

Key Responsibilities

  • Production order execution
  • Batch and lot tracking
  • Shop-floor monitoring and control
  • Production reporting and traceability
  • Communication with warehouse and automation systems

d. Warehouse Control and Automation Layer

This layer controls physical automation equipment on the warehouse floor.

System:

  • Cimcorp Warehouse Control System (WCS)

Key Responsibilities

  • Control of robotic palletizing systems
  • Conveyor and buffer management
  • Pallet creation and movement
  • Real-time equipment monitoring
  • Communication with MES for production and pallet data
  • (Note: although PI/PO is not the best option for S4HANA but as PI/PO was already using in ECC application, so used this one here)

4.0 Interesting facts about Tire:

Size of tire, which is imposed on tire physically, which is very much visible to all. This help to understand the exact width, Aspect ratio and Rim diameter for each tire.

 

 

And below summary, shows tire types, main features and major manufactures.

5.0 Event-Driven Integration Model:

5.1 Pallet build lifecycle:

5.1.1 Assumptions:

  • Each robot has a unique robot number and dedicated staging area
    • Each pallet has a unique pallet HU identifier
    • Each tire has a unique serial number encoded as a HU
    • Tiers with identical characteristics share the same material number
    • Each pallet has a predefined capacity stored within MES and WCS logic

5.1.2 Palletization steps:

 

Step 1: Tier Arrival Detection

A sensor or vision system detects tire arrival. The robot scans the pallet identifier and the tire serial number before pallet placement.

Step 2: Robotic Pallet Placement

The robot places tiers on the pallet following a predefined stacking pattern.

Step 3: WCS Event Recording

The WCS records the pallet build information including:

  • Pallet ID
    • Serial number(tyre serial)
    • Material number
    • Date and time
    • Quantity
  • Stock Type
  • Date of manufacturing
  • Date of expiry

 

Step 4: Pallet Completion

Once the pallet reaches the defined capacity, the robot moves the pallet to an outbound conveyor staging area. And then WCS send MES, pallet information and following this, MES updates this record and complete order. marks the pallet as complete in their record.

 

Step 5: XML Message Transmission

  • The pallet information is transmitted to MES and SAP S/4HANA using an XML interface. In this scenario, we are using the Push mechanism, wherein MES is sending Pallet file in XML format though middle layer(SAP PI/PO) then respective pallet information is saved in a file under Input folder.
  • From MES side: Once XML file goes to SAP/PI/PO then that file, moves from process folder to achieve folder
  • As soon as XML file saves in file under SAP, then parallel information goes to queue(can verify in SMQ2/SMQ1) and process the each pallet.

 

Example:

Step 6: Tire Handling Unit Creation

In SAP, each tire serial number is created as an individual handling unit with expiration characteristics (Added below fields in /SCWM/HUITM)
